payload.title: Define Narada secret management as capability-governed and locus-aware
payload.summary: Operator and architect identified that Narada's telos implies secret management should treat secrets as authority-bearing capabilities, not ordinary configuration or knowledge. Narada Sites may record that a secret exists and what capability it grants, but raw secret values should live in appropriate authority-bearing secret stores for the relevant locus.
evidence: Narada Sites are becoming plural, federated, Git-backed, pub/sub-connected, and agent-assisted; this increases risk if secrets are treated as portable Site knowledge.
User Site, PC Site, project/client Sites, data/ELT Sites, cloud Sites, and agents have different secret authority and storage requirements.
Plural Embodiment, Singular Authority implies that access to a secret-capability must resolve to a declared authority locus rather than whichever embodiment asks.
Verifiable envelope trust proposal covers message authenticity; secret management covers protected capabilities used to authenticate, decrypt, sign, fetch, execute, or mutate external systems.
proposal: Develop Narada secret-management doctrine around capabilities: knowledge can be copied, secrets confer power.
Site config should store secret references, metadata, intended capability, authority locus, allowed principals, and retrieval policy, not raw secret values.

Support locus-specific secret stores: Windows Credential Manager/DPAPI for PC or User loci, OS keychains, .env only as local development fallback, cloud provider secret stores, GitHub Actions secrets, client-managed vaults, and future Narada-managed adapters.
Separate secret discovery, authorization, retrieval, use, rotation, revocation, and audit as distinct governed transitions.
Agents should request capabilities by reference and purpose. Raw secret disclosure to an agent should be exceptional, explicit, auditable, and bounded by tool authority.

Secret references should be compatible with Site lifecycle, pub/sub, and cloning: cloned or re-instantiated Sites should carry references/policies, not secret material, and should require local binding or reauthorization.
doctor/preflight should detect raw secret leakage, missing local bindings, stale/rotated secrets, wrong-locus secret references, and unsafe Git/cloud-sync inclusion.
payload.recommendation: Promote to Narada security/authority design work before broad Site federation: define capability-oriented secret management with locus-aware stores, governed access, and audit.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
